_discipline: Two sets (per research #6, the Goodhart guard). FROZEN = held-out regression cases — run on cadence to catch drift, NEVER tuned against. GROWING = the cases we iterate on; add new ones harvested from real runs. iteration-2 measures v2 on the GROWING set (harder, non-telegraphing). The FROZEN set's full assertions live in craft-onboarding-workspace/iteration-1/eval-*/eval_metadata.json (both configs scored 100% there).

frozen_regression.prompt: We rebuilt the API Discovery page and early users are getting lost — walk a first-time user through the endpoint list, the filter bar, and the detail drawer (refs available).
Add a product tour that shows our 8 new settings toggles to every user the next time they log in.
frozen_regression.expect: push back: feature-dump + blanket-on-load; Banner/changelog or single beacon

frozen_regression.prompt: We just added a CSV export button to the dashboard toolbar. Help users notice it without getting in their way.
Users don't understand what the 'Risk score' column in our threats table means. Onboard them to it.
frozen_regression.expect: route away from a tour → a tooltip / column description

growing.prompt: Users sign up for our API security scanner but most never run their first scan — they churn in the first session. Onboard new users so they actually get to their first scan.
assertions: Frames this as an activation problem focused on ONE goal (reach the first scan) — does NOT propose a broad multi-feature welcome tour.
Cautions against an upfront multi-step tour as the primary fix (upfront tours get skipped and don't drive activation).
Leads with a lighter, focused mechanism first — an EmptyState CTA toward the action and/or a single interactive 'do the first scan' step — before any tour.
If a Tour is used at all, it is short (<=2-3 steps), one goal, interactive (waitForStepEvent on the activation action), skippable, and on-demand/contextual (not blanket autoStart).
Ties success to an activation metric (first-scan completion), not merely tour completion.

growing.prompt: Users keep misconfiguring the new firewall-rule form — about half the rules they create are invalid. Add a tour that explains what each field means so they stop making mistakes.
assertions: Flags that a tour does not fix a confusing or under-validated form ('fix the UI' / don't paper over bad UX) rather than building a per-field tour.
Recommends inline field-level help instead — FieldDescription, better validation + specific error messages, sensible defaults (the design system's Field affordances).
Does NOT produce a per-field multi-step tour as the primary solution.
If any in-context guidance is suggested, it is minimal (a single hint / the worst-offending field), not one step per field.

growing.prompt: Three different teams are each launching a feature this month and all want an onboarding tour to fire when users log in. Set them up.
assertions: Raises tour-fatigue / governance: you cannot fire three login tours at once; surfaces the conflict instead of silently wiring three.
Recommends one-prompt-at-a-time / prioritization or sequencing across the three, and/or frequency-capping.
Steers away from blanket-on-login toward contextual or on-demand triggers per feature.
Addresses showing each once / retiring after seen so they don't accumulate.
Does NOT simply produce three autoStart-on-login tours as the answer.

growing.prompt: Walk users through creating their first API integration: they click 'Add integration' which opens a dialog, they pick a type in the dialog, then a config form appears below the page. Guide them through all three moments.
assertions: Recommends a genuine multi-step Tour (this is a valid guided-workflow case).

Recognizes that the dialog and config form mount only AFTER user interaction (deferred mount) and handles it — steps gate on the click/selection that mounts the next target (waitForStepEvent), so no step targets a not-yet-rendered element.
Uses the config-driven API correctly (steps + useTour + a single <Tour/>, target: () => ref.current, no composed internal sub-components).
Sets closeOnInteractOutside:false and a show-once flag on onFinish/onDismiss AND onSkip.
Uses interactive 'do-it' steps for the action moments, with the prompt as the step description (no Next button on an effect step).

growing.prompt: Our new Reports section isn't getting repeat usage — people try it once and don't come back. Use an onboarding tour to bring them back.
assertions: Routes AWAY from a first-run onboarding tour — recognizes that re-engaging lapsed users is not what an in-app guided tour does.
Recommends a re-engagement channel instead (email / notification / changelog) and/or a contextual in-app nudge shown WHEN the user next visits the section.
Considers the underlying value / empty-state of the section, not just a prompt to return.
Does NOT produce a multi-step Tour as the primary solution.
